Merseyside In Progress

Cheshire In Progress
Resources Crisis Information

At risk right now?

If you feel like you might attempt suicide, or may have seriously harmed yourself, you need urgent medical help

Call 999

Mental health emergencies are serious. You're not wasting anyone's time.

Attend A&E

If your life is at risk, you need an emergency service. A&E departments are open 24 hours a day and anyone can visit them free of charge.

Call the Crisis team

Crisis teams support people who might otherwise need to go to hospital. They can support you during a crisis if you're already under their care.

Let's talk 💬

Whatever you're going through, there are people you can talk to any time.

  • Call Samaritans on 116 123 (UK-wide). You can also email jo@samaritans.org or visit some branches in person. You can also call the Samaritans Welsh Language Line on 0808 164 0123 (7pm–11pm every day).
  • Call Hopeline on 0800 068 4141 (Under 35s only, UK-wide) or text 0786 003 9967. You can also email pat@papyrus-uk.org
  • Text SHOUT to 85258 (UK-wide)
  • Call C.A.L.L. on 0800 132 737 (Wales only)

Other Services

  • Mind have a useful list of helplines and listening services
  • If you're homeless, Crisis can provide safe housing and advice
